title: Score an RTX 5070 prebuilt with a Ryzen 7 7700X & 16 GB of DDR5 for $650 off Stack these promo codes to save on Skytech Gaming's high-end systems
summary: Skytech Gaming offers prebuilt RTX 5070 systems with Ryzen 7 7700X and 16 GB DDR5 for a discounted price of $650 off. The Shadow 5 and Archangel 5 systems are available in black and white setups, respectively, and are designed to meet gaming needs. This makes high-end gaming more affordable for consumers.
insight: High-end gaming systems are now within budget-friendly reach.

title: Steam Controller leaked review points to $99 MSRP more expensive than PS5 and Xbox controllers and Nintendo Joy-Cons
summary: A leaked review of the Steam Controller revealed its $99 MSRP, making it more expensive than other popular gaming controllers.
insight: Gaming enthusiasts face higher costs with Steam's new controller option.

title: Intel's upcoming Xeon 7 "Diamond Rapids" server CPUs reportedly delayed to 2027 Next-gen Coral Rapids lineup lands 2028 but can be accelerated, according to new leak
summary: Intel's Xeon 7 'Diamond Rapids' CPU launch delayed to 2027, not this year as initially planned. The Clearwater Forest E-core-only CPU is still expected to release in the first half of 2024. The next-gen Coral Rapids family is set to arrive in 2028.
insight: Intel's server CPU roadmap faces significant delays and revisions.

title: Microsoft will allow users to indefinitely pause updates in Windows 11 first change in over a decade to the mandatory update policy
summary: Microsoft is giving Windows 11 users more control over updates, allowing them to pause indefinitely and restoring normal shutdown options even when updates are pending. This change aims to improve user experience and flexibility. Updates will also be made clearer and more transparent.
insight: Users regain control over Windows 11 updates for the first time

title: Maine governor vetoes bill that bans large new data centers says legislature should ve exempted one particular well-supported data center
summary: Maine Governor Janet Mills vetoed a data center moratorium bill, citing a specific project's impact.
insight: Economic development vs environmental concerns in data center regulation matters

title: Intel has reportedly cancelled discrete gaming GPUs for the upcoming Xe3P Arc "Celestial" family gaming GPU remains uncertain even for the next-gen Xe4 "Druid" lineup that lands in 2027
summary: Intel reportedly cancels discrete gaming GPUs for Xe3P, focuses on datacenter and mobile.
insight: Gaming GPU market absence hurts Intel's consumer segment growth opportunities.

title: Intel Core Ultra 7 270K Plus vs AMD Ryzen 7 7800X3D Can Intel finally beat X3D?
summary: Intel's Core Ultra 7 270K Plus challenges the AMD Ryzen 7 7800X3D in gaming benchmarks. The Core Ultra 7 270K Plus offers competitive performance in various tasks, but its gaming prowess is put to the test against the X3D. Our comparison highlights the strengths and weaknesses of each CPU.
insight: Intel's Core Ultra 7 270K Plus closes the gaming gap with X3D.

title: Your Walmart might be 3D printed firm building more than a dozen 3D-printed Walmart expansions with concrete-printing robots
summary: Alquist 3D is using its A1X robot to 3D print Walmart expansions with concrete at a high speed.
insight: Revolutionizing construction with faster, more efficient 3D printing technology.

title: Intel VP claims up to 30% of CPU performance is untapped by modern games software optimization is critical to unlocking full potential of hybrid CPUs
summary: Intel claims up to 30% of CPU performance is untapped due to suboptimal software, and better optimization can unlock its true potential. Hybrid architecture CPUs are capable but underutilized, and software updates can improve performance without hardware upgrades. This highlights the importance of software optimization for modern CPU architectures.
insight: Software optimization is key to unlocking hybrid CPU potential fully.

title: AMD's memory-boosting EXPO 1.2 is here, adds support for three Chinese memory vendors performance gains could be muted until Zen 6
summary: AMD EXPO 1.2 is a memory-boosting technology for AM5 motherboards, offering support for three Chinese memory vendors. It aims to improve performance, but gains may be muted until Zen 6. EXPO 1.2 is now available on select motherboards.
insight: Performance gains may be muted until Zen 6 architecture

title: Enthusiast fixes 30-year issue with S3 graphics card hacking the VBIOS fixes black levels by scalpelling out the Virge DX s pedestal bit
summary: A retro hardware enthusiast has found a fix for a 30-year issue with S3 graphics cards, eliminating the washed-out appearance caused by the Virge DX's pedestal bit. This was achieved by hacking the VBIOS to scalp out the problematic bit. The fix enables users to enjoy improved black levels on their retro gaming systems.
insight: Preserving retro gaming experiences with hardware tweaks matters greatly.

title: US Navy tests laser weapon that shoots down drones on the USS George H.W. Bush supercarrier system tracked, engaged, and neutralized multiple target drones, has essentially unlimited power source
summary: The US Navy tested the LOCUST Laser Weapon System on the USS George H.W. Bush, successfully neutralizing multiple target drones.
insight: Game-changing laser technology offers limitless power and precise drone destruction capability.

title: LightInk An ESP32-based, solar-powered E-ink smartwatch with up to 10 months of battery life
summary: LightInk is a solar-powered E-ink smartwatch with up to 10 months of battery life. It features a 1.54-inch e-paper display and supports various wireless connectivity options. The watch is built using an ESP32-based design with a low-quiescent-power system.
insight: Extremely low power consumption enables months-long battery life on small solar-powered battery

title: BMW brings color changing tech closer to production with the iX3 Flow Edition
summary: BMW has unveiled the iX3 Flow Edition, integrating E Ink's Prism technology into the vehicle's hood, bringing color-changing tech closer to production. This version embeds the electrophoretic system directly into the structure, moving beyond earlier external designs. The innovative design aims to revolutionize vehicle aesthetics.
insight: Embedded E Ink tech makes color-changing cars more practical

title: Windows Insiders can now pause updates indefinitely, in 35-day increments
summary: Windows Insiders can now pause updates indefinitely in 35-day increments, skip updates during initial setup, and reboot without installing updates.
insight: Increased user control over system updates brings greater flexibility and peace of mind.

title: The next Xbox could be waiting on the memory market
summary: The next Xbox's release might be impacted by memory market conditions. Microsoft aims to create a console capable of running great games and PC titles while considering market fluctuations. The company's decision-making process is driven by these factors.
insight: Memory costs drive Xbox development timeline uncertainty

title: Battlefield movie reportedly in the works with Christopher McQuarrie and Michael B. Jordan
summary: A Battlefield film adaptation is reportedly in the works with Christopher McQuarrie and Michael B. Jordan. McQuarrie will write, direct, and produce the project, while Jordan is in talks to produce. The film aims to bring the popular EA series to the big screen.
insight: High-profile collaboration could elevate Battlefield franchise's cinematic appeal.

title: Samsung phone division could post its first ever loss as AI drives memory costs higher
summary: Samsung's phone division might face its first loss due to rising memory costs, driven by AI's growing demand for DRAM and NAND.
insight: AI-driven demand boosts memory costs, threatening Samsung's profit margins.
